evidence: 83 tests passing, 0 failing
Ruff + pyright + coverage all enforced in CI (84% line coverage, 80% minimum)
Structured logging added throughout core.py (search, verify_claim, evidence_report, provider success/failure, circuit breaker trips) and browse_page.py (browse, browse_error)
Circuit breaker pattern with configurable thresholds and metadata reporting
Readability-style main content detection with text density, link density, semantic HTML5, ARIA role, and ID/class heuristics
Baseline comparison heuristics (majority-vote, keyword-count) in evidence_report output
Coverage.py with CI enforcement prevents regression of untested code
gaps: No async/await implementation; concurrent execution relies on threads
browse_page readability heuristic not validated against diverse real-world pages
axes.improvement_from_iteration_4: +1 point (23→24). Structured logging fills the production monitoring gap. Soria Parra's enriched profile provides deeper review coverage for MCP/protocol aspects.
axes.skeptic_challenge: Hanselowski (skeptic) notes: Scores above 24 need demonstrated production resilience under load. The logging is present but not yet validated in a production environment. The async gap remains.

evidence: Sub-claim targeted sub-queries: each sub-claim independently searches for its own evidence
Baseline comparison heuristics (majority-vote, keyword-count) provide transparency: users can see whether the weighted model adds value over simple baselines
Circuit breaker prevents cascading provider failures, maintaining search effectiveness under degraded conditions
Readability-style content extraction reduces boilerplate noise, improving page-aware verification quality
Structured logging enables production monitoring and debugging of verification pipelines
Coverage.py enforcement at 80% minimum prevents regression of effectiveness features
gaps: Claim decomposition is rule-based (regex) and does not handle complex sentence structures
No NLP-based stance detection or entailment reasoning
No confidence calibration against a gold-standard dataset
Baselines are reported but not benchmarked against a labeled dataset
axes.improvement_from_iteration_4: +1 point (23→24). Structured logging enables production debugging of effectiveness regressions. Soria Parra's primitive-usage review perspective adds depth to MCP tool effectiveness evaluation.

weighted_median_calculation_notes.normalization_note: Raw weights normalized to sum to 1.0

recommendations: DEPTH: Add more source reads for Nakov to increase source confidence above 0.75.
THICKNESS: Add async/await for provider calls to improve concurrent execution.
EFFECTIVENESS: Benchmark baseline heuristics against a labeled dataset to quantify weighted model improvement.
EFFECTIVENESS: Consider adding a simple zero-shot stance classifier as an optional enhancement.
BREADTH: Consider a production/SRE specialist for adversarial robustness and latency expertise.
